reason:
Both answers correctly answer "no," identify the CI/CD pipeline as the real bottleneck, recommend the same concrete fixes (continuous deploy, faster/parallelized CI, feature flags, automated rollback), point to modular monolith/Packwerk as the next step, and cite Shopify/GitHub as scale precedent. Content coverage is essentially equivalent.

Answer A is somewhat more explicit on the "extract only along real team/scaling seams" point ("Extract service only when one component need independent scaling or truly separate team") and gives a concrete quantitative heuristic for reconsidering microservices (~50+ engineers), plus a clear closing verdict/action plan. Answer B is more polished in prose and formatting (numbered list, specific tool name Knapsack Pro) but is thinner on the extraction-criteria and revisit-threshold points, mentioning modular monolith almost in passing.

Answer A has minor grammatical roughness (dropped articles, "engineer count triple") but this doesn't affect correctness or usefulness. Given the slightly stronger treatment of the team-size/extraction-seam key point, A has a small edge, though the two are close.
